top_categories.workout_context: 171 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
18,045ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Chasing Cancellara's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
At Chasing Cancellara, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Chasing Cancellara's fully paved alpine roads through Swiss mountain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 171 miles.
18,045ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
106ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 171 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Chasing Cancellara. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 18,045ft of climbing on fully paved alpine roads through Swiss mountain does to your power file on race day.
171 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Low-cadence force work for 18,045ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 171 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 171 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Chasing Cancellara. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Chasing Cancellara requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on fully paved alpine roads through swiss mountain passes to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, VO2max, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 171 miles of fully paved alpine roads through swiss mountain passes in Switzerland (multiple routes).
